title: Molecular and Cellular Biologists
description: Research and study cellular molecules and organelles to understand cell function and organization.

tasks.text: Maintain accurate laboratory records and data.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Design molecular or cellular laboratory experiments, oversee their execution, and interpret results.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write grant applications to obtain funding.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform laboratory procedures following protocols including deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) sequencing, cloning and extraction, ribonucleic acid (RNA) purification, or gel electrophoresis.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct research on cell organization and function, including mechanisms of gene expression, cellular bioinformatics, cell signaling, or cell differentiation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare or review reports, manuscripts, or meeting presentations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Instruct undergraduate and graduate students within the areas of cellular or molecular biology.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct, coordinate, organize, or prioritize biological laboratory activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compile and analyze molecular or cellular experimental data and adjust experimental designs as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Evaluate new technologies to enhance or complement current research.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Provide scientific direction for project teams regarding the evaluation or handling of devices, drugs, or cells for in vitro and in vivo disease models.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise technical personnel and postdoctoral research fellows.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor or operate specialized equipment, such as gas chromatographs and high pressure liquid chromatographs, electrophoresis units, thermocyclers, fluorescence activated cell sorters, and phosphorimagers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct applied research aimed at improvements in areas such as disease testing, crop quality, pharmaceuticals, and the harnessing of microbes to recycle waste.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop guidelines for procedures such as the management of viruses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop assays that monitor cell characteristics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate molecular or cellular research activities with scientists specializing in other fields.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Verify all financial, physical, and human resources assigned to research or development projects are used as planned.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Evaluate new supplies and equipment to ensure operability in specific laboratory settings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Participate in all levels of bioproduct development, including proposing new products, performing market analyses, designing and performing experiments, and collaborating with operations and quality control teams during product launches.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with vendors to evaluate new equipment or reagents or to discuss the customization of product lines to meet user requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Design databases, such as mutagenesis libraries.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
